sourceMovie.title: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) as a science fiction theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials clicked for you, Maze Runner: The Death Cure (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials?
faq.answer: Try Maze Runner: The Death Cure, Mad Max: Fury Road, The Maze Runner—each shares DNA with Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials?
faq.answer: Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials (2015) is best described as Science Fiction, Action, Thriller, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Maze Runner: The Scorch Trials part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Maze Runner Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
